data.narrative:
Mach Industries is a Series B-stage defense and security company that has raised $185M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparables universe of 10 peers—8 private companies and 2 public entities (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS)—all operating in the same sector and stage cohort. The private peer set includes Vannevar Labs, Dream Security, Harmattan AI, Code Metal, and Castelion, among others.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression modeling, using the $185M in total raised as a baseline and applying a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical for Series B advancement, implies a valuation of $352M. Peer-median analysis across the 8 same-sector, same-stage private comparables yields a median valuation of $1.1B, with a range spanning $300M to $1.5B. The disparity between methods reflects real variation in market pricing within the defense-tech space.

Triangulating these results produces a valuation range of $299M (low) to $1.3B (high), with a midpoint of $738M. The wide spread underscores material uncertainty around whether Mach should anchor to its capital-raised history or to peer market valuations. Key sensitivities include product maturity relative to the named comparables, customer concentration, and regulatory pathway clarity. Resolution of these factors would narrow the range meaningfully.
